stem: The population of a town was 3,500 in 1990 and 5,700 in 2010. If the relationship between the population and the year is linear, which of the following functions P models the population of the town t years after 1990?
rationale: Choice C is correct. The rate of change is (5700 - 3500)/(2010 - 1990) = 2200/20 = 110 per year, so the slope is 110. The starting value at t = 0 (year 1990) is 3500. Therefore P(t) = 110t + 3500. Verify: P(20) = 110(20) + 3500 = 2200 + 3500 = 5700 ✓. Choice A swaps the roles of slope and intercept. Choice B uses the wrong starting value. Choice D doubles the slope.
